message:
feat(demo_showcase): add E2E test logging and artifact capture infrastructure

Add comprehensive test support infrastructure for E2E testing:
- ScenarioRecorder: tracks events, inputs, assertions during test scenarios
- Structured event logging with JSON schema (ts, level, scenario, run_id, step, etc.)
- TestInput enum for key, mouse, resize, and paste events
- ConfigSnapshot for capturing seed, theme, and toggle settings
- Artifact output: events.jsonl, summary.txt, config.json, frames/*.txt
- Artifacts written only on failure (or with DEMO_SHOWCASE_KEEP_ARTIFACTS=1)
- Human-readable summary with failed assertion details and step timeline
- 8 comprehensive unit tests for the recorder functionality

Implements bd-299e.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-28jp","title":"demo_showcase UX: keybindings + interaction model","description":"Define an opinionated interaction model that feels premium:

- Global keys (examples):
  - `?` help overlay, `q` quit, `esc` back/close, `/` search/command palette, `t` theme switcher, `tab` focus next pane.
- Per-widget conventions: list/table navigation, viewport scrolling, form submit/cancel.
- Mouse expectations: click to focus/select; wheel scroll in viewports/lists.

Acceptance:
- A single canonical keymap that pages/components conform to (avoid per-page random keys).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.332580358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185343532Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:08:24.185278631Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:01.082895581Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28jp","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:05.356173194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28l3","title":"Wizard: implement huh form UI embedded in app","description":"Implement the Wizard page using huh components, embedded inside the demo_showcase app chrome.

Why:
- This is the "do the thing" workflow: it makes the demo feel like a real deployment/onboarding tool.
- It showcases huh’s strengths: multi-step forms, validation, confirm/select flows.

Requirements:
- Multi-step flow as designed in `Wizard: design the multi-step workflow`:
  - a mix of input types (text, select, confirm)
  - validations with clear inline errors
- Navigation:
  - cancel/back behavior matches global UX rules (`esc` closes / backs out safely)
  - submit produces a clear success/failure state
- Presentation:
  - keyboard-first
  - mouse optional
  - visually cohesive with the app theme

Integration:
- On completion, the wizard triggers the shared domain action API (separate task) to create a deployment/job and emit logs/toasts.

Acceptance:
- Wizard feels like a real onboarding/deploy tool, not a demo form.
- Validation and recovery flows are polished (errors feel intentional).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.916099726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:59.740408640Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","forms","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ynf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:41.872484594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:44.500650602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:01.934899534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28l3","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:47.373969167Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28wz","title":"Lipgloss: implement partial border edges conformance","description":"Enable the border_partial_top_bottom conformance test by handling partial border edges in run_border_test. The Go API uses BorderTop/BorderBottom/BorderLeft/BorderRight to control which edges render. The Rust Style has these methods; we just need to apply them based on the test name.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.947027377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173555122Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:46:51.173490171Z","close_reason":"Implemented partial border edges support in lipgloss conformance tests. border_partial_top_bottom now passes. Lipgloss conformance is now 58/58 (was 57/58 with 1 skip).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28wz","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:43:48.960863628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-299e","title":"E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture","description":"Add an E2E logging + artifacts foundation so failures are instantly diagnosable.

Why:
- TUIs are notoriously hard to debug from CI logs alone.
- We want every failure to answer: "what inputs happened", "what screen did we see", "what did we assert".

Deliverables:
- A `TestLogger`/`ScenarioRecorder` that records *structured* events:
  - scenario name + run id
  - step index + human-readable step description
  - injected input (key/mouse/resize) including raw bytes when relevant
  - assertions performed + expected vs actual summaries
  - captured output snapshots (last N frames + final frame)
  - active Config snapshot (seed/theme/toggles, artifacts dir)
- Output formats:
  - `events.jsonl` (machine readable) with stable keys for tooling
  - `summary.txt` (human readable) that points to the exact failing step
- Artifact directory structure (deterministic and CI-friendly):
  - `target/demo_showcase_e2e/<scenario>/<run_id>/...`
  - always write artifacts on failure; optionally keep them on success behind an env flag

Logging schema guidance (keep it simple and grep/jq-friendly):
- Required fields: `ts`, `level`, `scenario`, `run_id`, `step`, `event`, `message`
- Optional fields: `input`, `assertion`, `expected`, `actual`, `frame_path`, `config`

Acceptance:
- A failing E2E test leaves a clear trail: "what happened" + "what we expected" + the last visible screen.
- The artifact bundle is enough to reproduce locally (seed + scenario name + config snapshot).","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.556281120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:57:15.086284654Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-299e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.586010319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- The artifact bundle is enough to reproduce locally (seed + scenario name + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.556281120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301838829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:01:53.301768869Z","close_reason":"Implemented ScenarioRecorder with structured event logging, JSONL output, artifact capture, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-299e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:30.586010319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b7h","title":"Unit tests: simulation determinism (no real sleeps)","description":"Add unit tests proving the demo_showcase background simulation is deterministic and test-friendly.

Why:
- The demo depends on a "fake backend" simulation. If it’s nondeterministic, tests become flaky.
- E2E scenarios also rely on deterministic evolution (tick-driven, no real sleeps).

Coverage:
- Given a fixed seed and a fixed tick delta:
  - advancing N ticks produces expected state changes (jobs progress, metrics trends, log entries)
- No wall-clock sleeps:
  - the simulation can be stepped manually (pure tick function)
- Stability:
  - no NaNs/overflow/panics with extreme tick counts or dt values

Acceptance:
- The simulation is deterministic and easy to reason about.
- Tests run fast and do not require a TTY.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:06.578799968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528802419Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:27:15.528736406Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:06.591863269Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b7h","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:20.337957210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2eky","title":"demo_showcase core: guided tour / walkthrough mode","description":"High-impact demo feature: a guided walkthrough that showcases capabilities without the user needing prior knowledge.

Behavior:
- A key (e.g. `g`) starts a step-by-step tour.
- Each step highlights a UI region and prompts an action (or auto-navigates after a delay).
- Explains what the current screen demonstrates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, etc.).

Acceptance:
- A first-time user can run the tour and leave convinced this stack is real and powerful.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:22:54.239302138Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:23:04.532386317Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:23:02.079116118Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:22:54.254258582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:23:04.532354107Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eky","depends_on_id":"bd-zc72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:23:03.311920339Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2f52","title":"Unit tests: shell-out action safety (headless no-op, Cmd shape)","description":"Add unit tests for the demo_showcase shell-out capability.

Why:
- Shell-out is powerful but easy to make flaky (hanging pager, broken terminal restore).
- We need to guarantee CI/headless runs never block.

Test coverage:
- In headless/custom-IO mode (e.g., `--self-check`), triggering the shell-out action:
  - does *not* spawn a pager/process
  - returns quickly
  - emits a clear structured log/toast explaining it was skipped
- Cmd structure:
  - the action composes its work with `bubbletea::sequence(...)` so release/run/restore ordering is explicit
  - release + restore messages are present in the expected order when not headless

Acceptance:
- Tests run without a TTY and without external processes.
- Failures show exactly which invariant broke (ordering, headless guard, etc.).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:30.981035716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:31.002031497Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f52","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:31.002015177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f52","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:30.996944015Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
